title: Add PodDisruptionBudget to Keystone operator
description:
**Size:** 🔧 small
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** high
**Source:** Proposed for 'Implement PodDisruptionBudget
  - In reconcileDeplo'

Implement PDB support in the Keystone operator:

1. **`buildPodDisruptionBudget()`** in `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go`: Construct `policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget` named `{keystone.Name}-api`, using `commonLabels()` on ObjectMeta and `selectorLabels()` on `Spec.Selector.MatchLabels`. Use `minAvailable: 1` when `replicas > 1`, `maxUnavailable: 1` when `replicas == 1` to prevent drain deadlocks.

2. **`EnsurePDB()`** in `internal/common/deployment/deployment.go`: Reusable get-or-create/update helper following the existing `EnsureDeployment`/`EnsureService` contract — set owner reference via `SetControllerReference` on create, patch spec on update, return error only (no readiness bool). Use symmetric DeepCopy-then-normalize before spec comparison per codebase patterns.

3. **RBAC marker** on `Reconcile()` in `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go`: Add `+kubebuilder:rbac:groups=policy,resources=poddisruptionbudgets,verbs=get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete` alongside existing markers.

4. **`SetupWithManager` watch** in `keystone_controller.go`: Add `Owns(&policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget{})` so PDB changes trigger reconciliation.

5. **Import** `policyv1 "k8s.io/api/policy/v1"` in both controller and deployment helper files.

6. **Call site** in `reconcileDeployment`: Invoke `buildPodDisruptionBudget()` + `EnsurePDB()` after `EnsureService`, before setting the `DeploymentReady` condition.

7. **Tests** in `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go`: Add `policyv1` to `deployTestScheme()`. Test PDB creation (owner ref, labels, selector matches Deployment pod selector), correct `minAvailable`/`maxUnavailable` for replica counts >1 and ==1, and update-on-replica-change behavior. Follow existing Gomega BDD patterns with `NewGomegaWithT`.

**Rationale:** Keystone currently has no PodDisruptionBudget. During voluntary disruptions (kubectl drain, node upgrades, cluster maintenance), all Keystone API pods can be evicted simultaneously, causing a complete API outage. With a PDB, Kubernetes guarantees at least one pod remains available during rolling disruptions, providing zero-downtime resilience for the identity service that all other OpenStack components depend on.

**Affected Areas:**
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go
- internal/common/deployment/deployment.go

stories.title: Operator ensures PDB exists for Keystone API pods during reconciliation
stories.want: the Keystone operator to automatically create and manage a PodDisruptionBudget for the Keystone API deployment
stories.so_that: voluntary disruptions (node drains, upgrades) cannot evict all API pods simultaneously, preserving identity service availability
criteria: Running reconcileDeployment creates a PodDisruptionBudget named {keystone.Name}-api in the same namespace as the Keystone CR
PDB Spec.Selector.MatchLabels match selectorLabels(keystone) output, identical to the Deployment pod selector
PDB has an OwnerReference pointing to the Keystone CR with controller=true

stories.title: PDB uses minAvailable when replicas > 1 and maxUnavailable when replicas == 1
stories.want: the PDB to use the correct disruption strategy based on replica count
stories.so_that: single-replica deployments are not drain-deadlocked (minAvailable=1 blocks all evictions) and multi-replica deployments guarantee at least one pod remains available
criteria: When spec.replicas > 1, PDB sets minAvailable=1 and does NOT set maxUnavailable
When spec.replicas == 1, PDB sets maxUnavailable=1 and does NOT set minAvailable
Changing replicas from 1 to >1 (or vice versa) updates the PDB spec on next reconciliation

stories.title: PDB is updated when Keystone spec changes
stories.want: the operator to reconcile the PDB when the Keystone CR or PDB is modified externally
stories.so_that: the PDB always reflects the current desired state and manual edits are corrected
criteria: EnsurePDB updates the existing PDB spec when the desired spec differs from the live object
SetupWithManager includes Owns(&policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget{}) so PDB changes trigger reconciliation
RBAC markers grant get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete on policy/poddisruptionbudgets

stories.title: EnsurePDB is a reusable helper following the existing Ensure* contract
stories.want: a generic EnsurePDB helper in internal/common/deployment that other operators can reuse
stories.so_that: PDB management is consistent across all operators and follows the established get-or-create/update pattern
criteria: EnsurePDB lives in internal/common/deployment/deployment.go alongside EnsureDeployment and EnsureService
Signature: func EnsurePDB(ctx, client.Client, *runtime.Scheme, client.Object, *policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget) error
On create: sets OwnerReference via controllerutil.SetControllerReference, then creates
On update: replaces entire Spec field of existing PDB, then updates
Returns error only (no readiness bool), matching EnsureService contract

requirements.description: reconcileDeployment SHALL build and ensure a PodDisruptionBudget named {keystone.Name}-api after ensuring the Service and before evaluating the DeploymentReady condition
requirements.rationale: Core feature requirement: PDB must be created/updated as part of the deployment sub-reconciler flow

scenarios.name: PDB created on first reconciliation
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment runs and no PDB exists
scenarios.then: a PDB named {keystone.Name}-api is created in the Keystone namespace with correct labels and selector
and_then: PDB has OwnerReference to the Keystone CR

scenarios.name: PDB creation failure propagated
scenarios.when: EnsurePDB returns an error during reconcileDeployment
scenarios.then: reconcileDeployment returns (ctrl.Result{}, error) with wrapped error message 'ensuring PDB: ...'

scenarios.name: PDB ensured after Service before condition check
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment completes without error
scenarios.then: Deployment, Service, and PDB all exist in the namespace

requirements.description: buildPodDisruptionBudget SHALL set minAvailable=1 when replicas > 1 and maxUnavailable=1 when replicas == 1
requirements.rationale: Prevents drain deadlock: minAvailable=1 on a single-replica deployment blocks ALL evictions; maxUnavailable=1 allows the single pod to be evicted and rescheduled

scenarios.name: Multi-replica uses minAvailable
scenarios.when: keystone.Spec.Replicas is 3
scenarios.then: PDB Spec.MinAvailable is intstr.FromInt32(1) and Spec.MaxUnavailable is nil

scenarios.name: Single-replica uses maxUnavailable
scenarios.when: keystone.Spec.Replicas is 1
scenarios.then: PDB Spec.MaxUnavailable is intstr.FromInt32(1) and Spec.MinAvailable is nil

scenarios.name: Boundary: replicas == 2 uses minAvailable
scenarios.when: keystone.Spec.Replicas is 2
scenarios.then: PDB Spec.MinAvailable is intstr.FromInt32(1) and Spec.MaxUnavailable is nil

requirements.description: buildPodDisruptionBudget SHALL use commonLabels() on ObjectMeta.Labels and selectorLabels() on Spec.Selector.MatchLabels
requirements.rationale: Labels and selectors must be consistent with the Deployment for pod targeting and resource discoverability

scenarios.name: PDB labels match common labels
scenarios.when: buildPodDisruptionBudget is called
scenarios.then: PDB ObjectMeta.Labels equals commonLabels(keystone)

scenarios.name: PDB selector matches deployment pod selector
scenarios.when: buildPodDisruptionBudget and buildKeystoneDeployment are called with the same Keystone CR
scenarios.then: PDB Spec.Selector.MatchLabels equals Deployment Spec.Selector.MatchLabels

requirements.description: EnsurePDB SHALL follow the get-or-create/update contract: SetControllerReference on create, replace Spec on update, return error only
requirements.rationale: Matches EnsureService/EnsureCronJob pattern for consistency across the codebase

scenarios.name: Creates PDB with owner reference when not found
scenarios.when: EnsurePDB is called and the PDB does not exist
scenarios.then: SetControllerReference sets ownership, then Create succeeds, returns nil

scenarios.name: Updates existing PDB spec
scenarios.when: EnsurePDB is called and a PDB exists with a different spec
scenarios.then: existing.Spec is replaced with desired Spec, Update is called, returns nil

scenarios.name: Propagates Get errors
scenarios.when: client.Get returns a non-NotFound error
scenarios.then: EnsurePDB returns wrapped error with format 'getting PodDisruptionBudget %s/%s: %w'

scenarios.name: Propagates Create errors
scenarios.when: client.Create returns an error
scenarios.then: EnsurePDB returns wrapped error with format 'creating PodDisruptionBudget %s/%s: %w'

requirements.description: keystone_controller.go SHALL include an RBAC marker for policy/poddisruptionbudgets and an Owns watch for PodDisruptionBudget
requirements.rationale: RBAC is required for API access; Owns watch ensures PDB drift is corrected

scenarios.name: RBAC marker present
scenarios.when: controller-gen processes the RBAC markers
scenarios.then: generated role includes policy group, poddisruptionbudgets resource with get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete verbs

scenarios.name: Owns watch triggers reconciliation on PDB change
scenarios.when: an owned PDB is modified or deleted externally
scenarios.then: the owning Keystone CR is enqueued for reconciliation

requirements.description: policyv1 "k8s.io/api/policy/v1" SHALL be imported in reconcile_deployment.go, keystone_controller.go, and deployment.go
requirements.rationale: Go requires explicit imports for all referenced packages; missing imports cause compilation failure

scenarios.name: All three files compile with policyv1 import
scenarios.when: go build is run on the keystone operator and internal/common/deployment packages
scenarios.then: compilation succeeds with no missing import errors for policyv1 types

requirements.description: EnsurePDB unit tests SHALL cover create with owner ref, update, idempotency, and error scenarios in deployment_test.go
requirements.rationale: The reusable helper needs standalone test coverage independent of controller integration tests

scenarios.name: Create sets owner reference
scenarios.when: EnsurePDB is called with no existing PDB
scenarios.then: created PDB has OwnerReferences[0].Name == owner name, length 1

scenarios.name: Update changes spec
scenarios.when: EnsurePDB is called with existing PDB with different MinAvailable
scenarios.then: PDB spec is updated to match desired

scenarios.name: Idempotent operation
scenarios.when: EnsurePDB is called twice with same desired PDB
scenarios.then: no error, exactly one PDB exists in namespace

requirements.description: Controller-level tests SHALL verify PDB creation, labels, selector, replica-based strategy selection, and update-on-replica-change
requirements.rationale: End-to-end verification that buildPodDisruptionBudget + EnsurePDB integrate correctly within reconcileDeployment

scenarios.name: PDB created with correct name and owner ref
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment runs with replicas=3
scenarios.then: PDB test-keystone-api exists with OwnerReference to test-keystone

scenarios.name: PDB minAvailable for replicas=3
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment runs with replicas=3
scenarios.then: PDB.Spec.MinAvailable == intstr.FromInt32(1), MaxUnavailable is nil

scenarios.name: PDB maxUnavailable for replicas=1
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment runs with single-replica Keystone
scenarios.then: PDB.Spec.MaxUnavailable == intstr.FromInt32(1), MinAvailable is nil

scenarios.name: PDB spec updated when replicas change
scenarios.when: replicas changes from 3 to 1 and reconcileDeployment re-runs
scenarios.then: PDB switches from minAvailable=1 to maxUnavailable=1

scenarios.name: PDB selector matches Deployment selector
scenarios.when: both Deployment and PDB are created
scenarios.then: PDB.Spec.Selector.MatchLabels == Deployment.Spec.Selector.MatchLabels

tasks.title: Add EnsurePDB helper and unit tests in internal/common/deployment (REQ-004, REQ-006, REQ-007)
tasks.description: In internal/common/deployment/deployment.go: (1) Add import policyv1 "k8s.io/api/policy/v1". (2) Add EnsurePDB function following the EnsureService/EnsureCronJob contract — signature: func EnsurePDB(ctx context.Context, c client.Client, scheme *runtime.Scheme, owner client.Object, pdb *policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget) error. Logic: Get existing by ObjectKey; if NotFound → SetControllerReference + Create; if found → replace existing.Spec = pdb.Spec then Update. Wrap all errors with fmt.Errorf context. In deployment_test.go: (3) Add policyv1 import and policyv1.AddToScheme to newScheme(). (4) Add testPDB() factory returning a PDB with MinAvailable=1 and selector. (5) Add tests: TestEnsurePDB_creates (verify owner ref), TestEnsurePDB_updatesExisting (change MinAvailable, verify updated), TestEnsurePDB_idempotent (call twice, one PDB exists). Follow existing TestEnsureService_* and TestEnsureDeployment_* patterns.

tasks.title: Add buildPodDisruptionBudget function and policyv1 import in reconcile_deployment.go (REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-006)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go: (1) Add policyv1 "k8s.io/api/policy/v1" to imports alongside existing appsv1/corev1 imports. (2) Add buildPodDisruptionBudget(keystone *keystonev1alpha1.Keystone) *policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget function after buildKeystoneService (after line 261). Name: fmt.Sprintf("%s-api", keystone.Name). Namespace: keystone.Namespace. Labels: commonLabels(keystone). Spec.Selector: &metav1.LabelSelector{MatchLabels: selectorLabels(keystone)}. Replica logic: replicas := int32(keystone.Spec.Replicas); if replicas > 1 { set MinAvailable = intstr.FromInt32(1) } else { set MaxUnavailable = intstr.FromInt32(1) }. Use intstr.IntOrString pointer for the set field, leave the other nil.

tasks.title: Add PDB call site in reconcileDeployment (REQ-001)
tasks.description: In reconcileDeployment function (line 83, after EnsureService block): Add pdb := buildPodDisruptionBudget(keystone) followed by if err := deployment.EnsurePDB(ctx, r.Client, r.Scheme, keystone, pdb); err != nil { return ctrl.Result{}, fmt.Errorf("ensuring PDB: %w", err) }. This goes after the Service ensure block (line 82) and before the if !ready check (line 84).

tasks.title: Add RBAC marker, policyv1 import, and Owns watch in keystone_controller.go (REQ-005, REQ-006)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go: (1) Add policyv1 "k8s.io/api/policy/v1" to the import block after batchv1 import. (2) Add RBAC marker after line 56: // +kubebuilder:rbac:groups=policy,resources=poddisruptionbudgets,verbs=get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete. (3) In SetupWithManager, add .Owns(&policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget{}) after .Owns(&batchv1.CronJob{}) (after line 152).

tasks.title: Add PDB tests in reconcile_deployment_test.go (REQ-008)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go: (1) Add policyv1 "k8s.io/api/policy/v1" to imports. (2) Add _ = policyv1.AddToScheme(s) to deployTestScheme(). (3) Add test TestReconcileDeployment_PDBCreated: run reconcileDeployment with deployTestKeystone (replicas=3), verify PDB test-keystone-api exists via client.Get, check OwnerReferences has len 1 with Name test-keystone. (4) Add TestReconcileDeployment_PDBLabelsAndSelector: verify PDB labels match commonLabels, PDB Spec.Selector.MatchLabels match selectorLabels, and PDB selector equals Deployment selector. (5) Add TestReconcileDeployment_PDBMinAvailableForMultipleReplicas: with replicas=3 verify MinAvailable=intstr.FromInt32(1) and MaxUnavailable is nil. (6) Add TestReconcileDeployment_PDBMaxUnavailableForSingleReplica: create Keystone with Replicas=1, verify MaxUnavailable=intstr.FromInt32(1) and MinAvailable is nil. (7) Add TestReconcileDeployment_PDBUpdatedOnReplicaChange: first reconcile with replicas=3, then change ks.Spec.Replicas=1, re-run reconcileDeployment, verify PDB switched from minAvailable to maxUnavailable. (8) Add TestBuildPodDisruptionBudget_BoundaryReplicas2: replicas=2, verify minAvailable=1. (9) Add TestReconcileDeployment_PDBSelectorMatchesDeployment: verify PDB and Deployment selector labels are identical. Follow existing Gomega BDD patterns with NewGomegaWithT(t).

test_specifications.story: EnsurePDB is a reusable helper following the existing Ensure* contract
test_specifications.expected: When PDB exists with different spec, EnsurePDB updates the spec to match desired

test_specifications.story: EnsurePDB is a reusable helper following the existing Ensure* contract
test_specifications.expected: Calling EnsurePDB twice with same input succeeds with exactly one PDB in namespace

test_specifications.story: Operator ensures PDB exists for Keystone API pods during reconciliation
test_specifications.expected: After reconcileDeployment, PDB test-keystone-api exists with correct OwnerReference to test-keystone

test_specifications.story: Operator ensures PDB exists for Keystone API pods during reconciliation
test_specifications.expected: PDB labels match commonLabels, PDB selector matches Deployment selector (selectorLabels)

test_specifications.story: PDB uses minAvailable when replicas > 1 and maxUnavailable when replicas == 1
test_specifications.expected: With replicas=3, PDB.Spec.MinAvailable == intstr.FromInt32(1) and MaxUnavailable is nil

test_specifications.story: PDB uses minAvailable when replicas > 1 and maxUnavailable when replicas == 1
test_specifications.expected: With replicas=1, PDB.Spec.MaxUnavailable == intstr.FromInt32(1) and MinAvailable is nil

test_specifications.story: PDB is updated when Keystone spec changes
test_specifications.expected: Changing replicas from 3 to 1 and re-running reconcileDeployment switches PDB from minAvailable=1 to maxUnavailable=1

test_specifications.story: Operator ensures PDB exists for Keystone API pods during reconciliation

test_specifications.story: PDB uses minAvailable when replicas > 1 and maxUnavailable when replicas == 1
test_specifications.expected: With replicas=2, PDB uses minAvailable=1 (boundary: smallest multi-replica value)

review_criteria: go build ./... succeeds (compilation) — policyv1 imports present in all 3 files, no missing type references
go vet ./... passes with no warnings on keystone operator and internal/common/deployment packages
All existing tests pass (go test ./operators/keystone/... and go test ./internal/common/deployment/...) — no regressions
All new tests pass: EnsurePDB unit tests in deployment_test.go and PDB integration tests in reconcile_deployment_test.go

EnsurePDB follows the same get-or-create/update contract as EnsureService: SetControllerReference on create, replace Spec on update, error-only return, wrapped error messages with namespace/name context
RBAC marker on Reconcile() includes groups=policy,resources=poddisruptionbudgets with full CRUD verbs; SetupWithManager includes Owns(&policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget{})
PDB is named {keystone.Name}-api matching Deployment and Service naming convention
PDB uses minAvailable=1 when replicas > 1 and maxUnavailable=1 when replicas == 1 — no drain deadlock scenario
PDB labels use commonLabels(), PDB selector uses selectorLabels() matching Deployment pod selector exactly
Call site is after EnsureService and before DeploymentReady condition check in reconcileDeployment
implementation_notes:
Architecture: This feature adds PodDisruptionBudget management to the existing reconcileDeployment sub-reconciler. No new sub-reconciler or condition type is needed — PDB is an operational resource managed alongside the Deployment and Service.

Data flow: Keystone CR spec.replicas → buildPodDisruptionBudget() constructs PDB object → EnsurePDB() creates or updates in cluster → Kubernetes scheduler respects PDB during voluntary disruptions.

Key design decisions:
1. EnsurePDB returns error only (not bool), matching EnsureService — PDB has no readiness concept.
2. PDB spec is always replaced entirely (existing.Spec = pdb.Spec) matching the codebase pattern in EnsureCronJob. No server-assigned fields to preserve (unlike Service ClusterIP).
3. minAvailable vs maxUnavailable logic prevents drain deadlock: a PDB with minAvailable=1 on a 1-replica deployment would block all evictions since Kubernetes cannot guarantee availability while evicting the only pod.
4. Feature comment: // Feature: CC-0037 at the top of new functions.

Files modified:
- internal/common/deployment/deployment.go — Add policyv1 import + EnsurePDB function (follows EnsureCronJob pattern from internal/common/job/job.go:110-138)
- internal/common/deployment/deployment_test.go — Add policyv1 scheme registration + EnsurePDB unit tests (follows TestEnsureService_* pattern)
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go — Add policyv1 import + buildPodDisruptionBudget function + call site in reconcileDeployment
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go — Add policyv1 import + RBAC marker + Owns watch
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go — Add policyv1 scheme registration + PDB test cases

Existing patterns to follow:
- Builder: buildKeystoneService() at reconcile_deployment.go:245-261 — returns *Type without creating
- Ensure: EnsureCronJob() at job/job.go:110-138 — simplest error-only ensure pattern (no server-assigned field preservation)
- Tests: TestEnsureService_creates at deployment_test.go:231-249 — fake client, owner ref verification
- Controller tests: TestReconcileDeployment_DeploymentAndServiceCreated at reconcile_deployment_test.go:106-128
